notes: Chapter is an eschatological vision narrative (Second Coming, resurrection, glorification of the redeemed, Adam's restoration). The overwhelming majority of substantive claims are theological/interpretive per CLAUDE.md rule 5 (supernatural/future-prophetic content is unverifiable by definition) and are logged with needs_verification:false. The checkable layer consists of: (1) dozens of Scripture quotations, most carrying the chapter's own footnote markers (1091)-(1130), extracted as attributed_quote/needs_verification:true for quote-fidelity checking against the cited/identifiable Bible verse; (2) a handful of claims that restate specific Gospel narrative events (Herod's mockery, the crown of thorns, the nail-prints, the parable of the husbandmen, Stephen's vision, Moses' shining face, the giving of the law at Sinai, Christ's ascension) as historical events, flagged event/biographical/needs_verification:true; (3) one biographical/statistic claim about Adam's lifespan (~1000 years) checkable against Genesis 5; (4) one sweeping historical statistic ('Millions went down to the grave...') flagged for verification despite being effectively unquantifiable. Footnote numbers and likely underlying Bible references are recorded in extractor_note where identifiable; these attributions are the extractor's contextual identification, not confirmed verification.

claims.quote: When the protection of human laws shall be withdrawn from those who honor the law of God, there will be, in different lands, a simultaneous movement for their destruction.
claims.claim_text: The chapter predicts that when human legal protection is withdrawn from Sabbath-keepers, there will be a simultaneous movement in different lands to destroy them.

claims.quote: As the time appointed in the decree draws near, the people will conspire to root out the hated sect.
claims.claim_text: As the time appointed in a future decree draws near, the general populace will conspire to root out the persecuted Sabbath-keeping group.

claims.quote: It will be determined to strike in one night a decisive blow, which shall utterly silence the voice of dissent and reproof.
claims.claim_text: It will be determined to strike, in a single night, a decisive blow intended to utterly silence the voice of dissent and reproof from God's people.

claims.quote: The people of God—some in prison cells, some hidden in solitary retreats in the forests and the mountains—still plead for divine protection, while in every quarter companies of armed men, urged on by hosts of evil angels, are preparing for the work of death.
claims.claim_text: At this future crisis, God's people, some imprisoned and some hidden in forests and mountains, plead for divine protection while armed men, urged on by evil angels, prepare to kill them.
claims.extractor_note: Includes supernatural agency (evil angels) alongside the future-historical prediction; treated as a single theological claim.

claims.quote: It is now, in the hour of utmost extremity, that the God of Israel will interpose for the deliverance of His chosen.
claims.claim_text: At the hour of utmost extremity, God will interpose to deliver His chosen people.

claims.quote: Saith the Lord: “Ye shall have a song, as in the night when a holy solemnity is kept; and gladness of heart, as when one goeth ... to come into the mountain of Jehovah, to the Mighty One of Israel. And the Lord shall cause His glorious voice to be heard, and shall show the lighting down of His arm, with the indignation of His anger, and with the flame of a devouring fire, with scattering, and tempest, and hailstones.”
claims.claim_text: The text attributes to 'the Lord' a quotation promising a song of holy solemnity, God's glorious voice, and the descent of His arm with anger, devouring fire, scattering, tempest, and hailstones.

claims.quote: Then a rainbow, shining with the glory from the throne of God, spans the heavens, and seems to encircle each praying company.
claims.claim_text: In the vision, a rainbow shining with the glory of God's throne spans the heavens and appears to encircle each company of praying believers.

claims.quote: With shouts of triumph, jeering, and imprecation, throngs of evil men are about to rush upon their prey, when, lo, a dense blackness, deeper than the darkness of the night, falls upon the earth.
claims.claim_text: As throngs of evil men are about to rush upon their prey, a dense blackness, deeper than ordinary night, falls upon the earth and arrests the angry multitudes.

claims.quote: By the people of God a voice, clear and melodious, is heard, saying, “Look up,” and lifting their eyes to the heavens, they behold the bow of promise.
claims.claim_text: God's people hear a voice saying 'Look up' and, lifting their eyes, behold the bow of promise in the sky.

claims.quote: and like Stephen they look up steadfastly into heaven, and see the glory of God, and the Son of man seated upon His throne.
claims.claim_text: The chapter compares God's people at this moment to Stephen, who looked up steadfastly into heaven and saw the glory of God and the Son of man.
claims.extractor_note: Alludes to the account of Stephen's martyrdom vision in Acts 7:55-56.

claims.quote: and from His lips they hear the request, presented before His Father and the holy angels, “I will that they also, whom Thou hast given Me, be with Me where I am.”
claims.claim_text: The text attributes to Jesus, addressed to His Father and the holy angels, the words 'I will that they also, whom Thou hast given Me, be with Me where I am.'

claims.quote: Again a voice, musical and triumphant, is heard, saying: “They come! they come! holy, harmless, and undefiled. They have kept the word of My patience; they shall walk among the angels;”
claims.claim_text: A triumphant voice in the vision declares 'They come! they come! holy, harmless, and undefiled. They have kept the word of My patience; they shall walk among the angels.'
claims.extractor_note: Not carrying one of the chapter's numbered footnotes; presented as part of White's own composed vision narrative rather than a discrete Scripture citation.

claims.quote: It is at midnight that God manifests His power for the deliverance of His people.
claims.claim_text: According to the vision, God manifests His power for the deliverance of His people at midnight.

claims.quote: whence comes the voice of God like the sound of many waters, saying, “It is done.”
claims.claim_text: The text attributes to the voice of God, sounding like many waters, the words 'It is done.'

claims.quote: That voice shakes the heavens and the earth. There is a mighty earthquake, “such as was not since men were upon the earth, so mighty an earthquake, and so great.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes a description of a mighty earthquake 'such as was not since men were upon the earth, so mighty an earthquake, and so great.'

claims.quote: Babylon the Great has come in remembrance before God, “to give unto her the cup of the wine of the fierceness of His wrath.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that 'Babylon the Great' comes into remembrance before God so that she is given 'the cup of the wine of the fierceness of His wrath.'

claims.quote: Great hailstones, every one “about the weight of a talent,” are doing their work of destruction.
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that great hailstones, each about the weight of a talent, cause destruction.
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to Revelation 16:21; not marked with one of the chapter's numbered footnotes in this instance.

claims.quote: Mountain chains are sinking. Inhabited islands disappear. The seaports that have become like Sodom for wickedness, are swallowed up by the angry waters.
claims.claim_text: In the vision, mountain chains sink, inhabited islands disappear, and wicked seaports are swallowed by the sea.

claims.quote: Prison walls are rent asunder, and God’s people, who have been held in bondage for their faith, are set free.
claims.claim_text: In the vision, prison walls are torn apart and God's people, held in bondage for their faith, are set free.

claims.quote: Graves are opened, and “many of them that sleep in the dust of the earth ... awake, some to everlasting life, and some to shame and everlasting contempt.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that graves open and many who sleep in the dust of the earth awake, some to everlasting life and some to shame and everlasting contempt.

claims.quote: “They also which pierced Him,” those that mocked and derided Christ’s dying agonies, and the most violent opposers of His truth and His people, are raised to behold Him in His glory, and to see the honor placed upon the loyal and obedient.
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'they also which pierced Him' in describing that Christ's dying-agony mockers and violent opposers are resurrected to behold Him in His glory.

claims.quote: All who have died in the faith of the third angel’s message come forth from the tomb glorified, to hear God’s covenant of peace with those who have kept His law.
claims.claim_text: All who died holding the Adventist 'third angel's message' are resurrected glorified at this point in the vision.

claims.quote: Demons acknowledge the deity of Christ, and tremble before His power, while men are supplicating for mercy, and groveling in abject terror.
claims.claim_text: In the vision, demons acknowledge Christ's deity and tremble before His power while men beg for mercy in terror.

claims.quote: Said the prophets of old, as they beheld in holy vision the day of God: “Howl ye; for the day of the Lord is at hand; it shall come as a destruction from the Almighty.”
claims.claim_text: The text attributes to 'the prophets of old' the quotation 'Howl ye; for the day of the Lord is at hand; it shall come as a destruction from the Almighty.'

claims.quote: “Enter into the rock, and hide thee in the dust, for fear of the Lord, and for the glory of His majesty. The lofty looks of man shall be humbled, and the haughtiness of men shall be bowed down, and the Lord alone shall be exalted in that day. For the day of the Lord of hosts shall be upon every one that is proud and lofty, and upon every one that is lifted up; and he shall be brought low.” “In that day a man shall cast the idols of his silver, and the idols of his gold, which they made each one for himself to worship, to the moles and to the bats; to go into the clefts of the rocks, and into the tops of the ragged rocks, for fear of the Lord, and for the glory of His majesty, when He ariseth to shake terribly the earth.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes a prophetic passage about men hiding from the Lord's glory, the humbling of the proud, and men casting away their idols of silver and gold when the Lord arises to shake the earth.
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (1099); corresponds to a composite of Isaiah 2:10, 2:12, 2:19-21.

claims.quote: Through a rift in the clouds, there beams a star whose brilliancy is increased fourfold in contrast with the darkness.
claims.claim_text: In the vision, a star of fourfold brilliancy beams through a rift in the clouds.

claims.quote: Their voices rise in triumphant song: “God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble. Therefore will not we fear, though the earth be removed, and though the mountains be carried into the midst of the sea; though the waters thereof roar and be troubled, though the mountains shake with the swelling thereof.”
claims.claim_text: The delivered faithful are described singing the quoted words 'God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble... though the mountains shake with the swelling thereof.'

claims.quote: Then there appears against the sky a hand holding two tables of stone folded together.
claims.claim_text: In the vision, a hand holding two folded tables of stone appears against the sky.

claims.quote: Says the prophet, “The heavens shall declare His righteousness: for God is judge Himself.”
claims.claim_text: The text attributes to 'the prophet' the quotation 'The heavens shall declare His righteousness: for God is judge Himself.'

claims.quote: That holy law, God’s righteousness, that amid thunder and flame was proclaimed from Sinai as the guide of life, is now revealed to men as the rule of judgment.
claims.claim_text: The Ten Commandments were originally proclaimed from Mount Sinai amid thunder and flame.
claims.extractor_note: References the giving of the Decalogue at Sinai as narrated in Exodus 19-20.

claims.quote: “Then shall ye return, and discern between the righteous and the wicked, between him that serveth God and him that serveth Him not.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'Then shall ye return, and discern between the righteous and the wicked, between him that serveth God and him that serveth Him not.'

claims.quote: The Lord gave them His law; they might have compared their characters with it, and learned their defects while there was yet opportunity for repentance and reform; but in order to secure the favor of the world, they set aside its precepts and taught others to transgress.
claims.claim_text: The wicked had opportunity for repentance by comparing their characters to God's law, but set aside its precepts and taught others to transgress in order to secure the world's favor.

claims.quote: Too late they see that the Sabbath of the fourth commandment is the seal of the living God.
claims.claim_text: The claim that the Sabbath of the fourth commandment is the seal of the living God.

claims.quote: Religious teachers have led souls to perdition while professing to guide them to the gates of Paradise.
claims.claim_text: Religious teachers, professing to guide souls to Paradise, have instead led them to perdition.

claims.quote: The voice of God is heard from heaven, declaring the day and hour of Jesus’ coming, and delivering the everlasting covenant to His people.
claims.claim_text: In the vision, the voice of God from heaven declares the day and hour of Jesus' coming and delivers the everlasting covenant to His people.

claims.quote: Their countenances are lighted up with His glory, and shine as did the face of Moses when he came down from Sinai.
claims.claim_text: Moses' face shone with light when he came down from Mount Sinai.

claims.quote: Soon there appears in the east a small black cloud, about half the size of a man’s hand.
claims.claim_text: In the vision, a small black cloud, about half the size of a man's hand, appears in the east as the sign of the Son of man.
claims.extractor_note: Echoes the cloud imagery of 1 Kings 18:44 (Elijah), applied here without a footnote as part of the vision narrative.

claims.quote: “Faithful and true,” “in righteousness He doth judge and make war.” And “the armies which were in heaven follow Him.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'Faithful and true,' 'in righteousness He doth judge and make war,' and 'the armies which were in heaven follow Him' as describing Christ's return.

claims.quote: “His glory covered the heavens, and the earth was full of His praise. And His brightness was as the light.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'His glory covered the heavens, and the earth was full of His praise. And His brightness was as the light.'

claims.quote: “And He hath on His vesture and on His thigh a name written, King of kings, and Lord of lords.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'And He hath on His vesture and on His thigh a name written, King of kings, and Lord of lords.'

claims.quote: Before His presence, “all faces are turned into paleness;” upon the rejecters of God’s mercy falls the terror of eternal despair. “The heart melteth, and the knees smite together,” “and the faces of them all gather blackness.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that before Christ's presence 'all faces are turned into paleness,' 'the heart melteth, and the knees smite together,' and 'the faces of them all gather blackness.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (1106); corresponds to Jeremiah 30:6 and Nahum 2:10.

claims.quote: Then the voice of Jesus is heard, saying, “My grace is sufficient for you.”
claims.claim_text: The text attributes to Jesus, in the vision, the words 'My grace is sufficient for you.'
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to 2 Corinthians 12:9; no numbered footnote given at this instance.

claims.quote: “Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence: a fire shall devour before Him, and it shall be very tempestuous round about Him. He shall call to the heavens from above, and to the earth, that He may judge His people.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'Our God shall come, and shall not keep silence: a fire shall devour before Him... that He may judge His people.'

claims.quote: “And the kings of the earth, and the great men, and the rich men, and the chief captains, and the mighty men, and every bondman, and every freeman, hid themselves in the dens and in the rocks of the mountains; and said to the mountains and rocks, Fall on us, and hide us from the face of Him that sitteth on the throne, and from the wrath of the Lamb: for the great day of His wrath is come; and who shall be able to stand?”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that kings, great men, rich men, chief captains, mighty men, and every bondman and freeman hid themselves in dens and rocks, begging the mountains to fall on them and hide them from the wrath of the Lamb.

claims.quote: The clash of arms, the tumult of battle, “with confused noise, and garments rolled in blood,” is stilled.
claims.claim_text: The text quotes the phrase 'with confused noise, and garments rolled in blood' in describing the stilling of battle.

claims.quote: The cry bursts forth from lips so lately scoffing, “The great day of His wrath is come; and who shall be able to stand?”
claims.claim_text: The text describes the wicked, who had lately been scoffing, crying out 'The great day of His wrath is come; and who shall be able to stand?'
claims.extractor_note: Repeats the Revelation 6:17 wording already logged in claim GC-40-044 (footnote 1108), here narrated as the wicked's own reaction rather than as the source citation itself.

claims.quote: as that voice which has so long pleaded, “Turn ye, turn ye from your evil ways; for why will ye die?”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes the voice of Christ as having long pleaded 'Turn ye, turn ye from your evil ways; for why will ye die?'

claims.quote: Says Jesus: “I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out My hand, and no man regarded; but ye have set at naught all My counsel, and would none of My reproof.”
claims.claim_text: The text attributes to Jesus the quotation 'I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out My hand, and no man regarded; but ye have set at naught all My counsel, and would none of My reproof.'

claims.quote: With thrilling power come to their minds the Sufferer’s words, when, adjured by the high priest, He solemnly declared, “Hereafter shall ye see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.”
claims.claim_text: Jesus, when adjured by the high priest, declared 'Hereafter shall ye see the Son of man sitting on the right hand of power, and coming in the clouds of heaven.'

claims.quote: There is the haughty Herod who jeered at His royal title, and bade the mocking soldiers crown Him king.
claims.claim_text: Herod jeered at Jesus' royal title and had mocking soldiers crown Him king.

claims.quote: There are the very men who with impious hands placed upon His form the purple robe, upon His sacred brow the thorny crown, and in His unresisting hand the mimic scepter, and bowed before Him in blasphemous mockery.
claims.claim_text: Soldiers placed a purple robe, a crown of thorns, and a mock scepter on Jesus and bowed before Him in mockery.

claims.quote: The men who smote and spit upon the Prince of life, now turn from His piercing gaze, and seek to flee from the overpowering glory of His presence.
claims.claim_text: Men struck and spat upon Jesus during His trial/passion.

claims.quote: Those who drove the nails through His hands and feet, the soldier who pierced His side, behold these marks with terror and remorse.
claims.claim_text: Soldiers drove nails through Jesus' hands and feet, and a soldier pierced His side with a spear.
claims.extractor_note: References the crucifixion nailing and John 19:34 (the piercing of Jesus' side).

claims.quote: With shuddering horror they remember how, wagging their heads in satanic exultation, they exclaimed: “He saved others; Himself He cannot save. If He be the King of Israel, let Him now come down from the cross, and we will believe Him. He trusted in God; let Him deliver Him now, if He will have Him.”
claims.claim_text: Priests and rulers, wagging their heads at the cross, said of Jesus 'He saved others; Himself He cannot save. If He be the King of Israel, let Him now come down from the cross, and we will believe Him. He trusted in God; let Him deliver Him now, if He will have Him.'

claims.quote: Vividly they recall the Saviour’s parable of the husbandmen who refused to render to their lord the fruit of the vineyard, who abused his servants and slew his son.
claims.claim_text: Jesus told a parable of husbandmen who refused to render to their lord the fruit of the vineyard, abused his servants, and killed his son.
claims.extractor_note: References the parable of the wicked husbandmen, Matthew 21:33-41 (also Mark 12:1-9, Luke 20:9-16).

claims.quote: They remember, too, the sentence which they themselves pronounced: The lord of the vineyard “will miserably destroy those wicked men.”
claims.claim_text: In the parable, when asked what the lord of the vineyard would do, the chief priests/elders answered that he 'will miserably destroy those wicked men.'

claims.quote: Louder than the shout, “Crucify Him! crucify Him!” which rang through the streets of Jerusalem, swells the awful, despairing wail, “He is the Son of God! He is the true Messiah!”
claims.claim_text: A crowd in Jerusalem shouted 'Crucify Him! crucify Him!' regarding Jesus.
claims.extractor_note: References the Gospel crowd scenes, e.g. Luke 23:21, John 19:6,15.

claims.quote: But what are these compared with the remorse of that day when “fear cometh as desolation,” when “destruction cometh as a whirlwind”!
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'fear cometh as desolation' and 'destruction cometh as a whirlwind' to describe the remorse of the wicked at that day.

claims.quote: In the midst of their terror they hear the voices of the saints in joyful strains exclaiming, “Lo, this is our God; we have waited for Him, and He will save us.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes the saints exclaiming 'Lo, this is our God; we have waited for Him, and He will save us.'

claims.quote: He looks upon the graves of the righteous, then raising His hands to heaven He cries, “Awake, awake, awake, ye that sleep in the dust, and arise!”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes the Son of God calling, over the graves of the righteous, 'Awake, awake, awake, ye that sleep in the dust, and arise!'
claims.extractor_note: Echoes Isaiah 26:19; no numbered footnote at this instance.

claims.quote: From the prison-house of death they come, clothed with immortal glory, crying, “O death, where is thy sting? O grave, where is thy victory?”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes the resurrected as crying 'O death, where is thy sting? O grave, where is thy victory?'

claims.quote: Adam, who stands among the risen throng, is of lofty height and majestic form, in stature but little below the Son of God. He presents a marked contrast to the people of later generations; in this one respect is shown the great degeneracy of the race.
claims.claim_text: The resurrected Adam is of lofty height and majestic form, only slightly below the Son of God in stature, in contrast to later generations who show the physical degeneracy of the human race.

claims.quote: Restored to the tree of life in the long-lost Eden, the redeemed will “grow up” to the full stature of the race in its primeval glory.
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that the redeemed, restored to the tree of life in Eden, will 'grow up' to the full stature of the race in its primeval glory.
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (1117); the phrase 'grow up' echoes Malachi 4:2.

claims.quote: Christ’s faithful ones will appear “in the beauty of the Lord our God,” in mind and soul and body reflecting the perfect image of their Lord.
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that Christ's faithful ones will appear 'in the beauty of the Lord our God.'
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to Psalm 90:17; no numbered footnote at this instance.

claims.quote: The living righteous are changed “in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that the living righteous are changed 'in a moment, in the twinkling of an eye.'
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to 1 Corinthians 15:52; no numbered footnote at this instance.

claims.quote: Angels “gather together the elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that angels 'gather together the elect from the four winds, from one end of heaven to the other.'
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to Matthew 24:31; no numbered footnote at this instance.

claims.quote: as the chariot rolls upward, the wheels cry, “Holy,” and the wings, as they move, cry, “Holy,” and the retinue of angels cry, “Holy, holy, holy, Lord God Almighty.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes the wheels, wings, and angels around the ascending chariot crying 'Holy,' 'Holy,' and 'Holy, holy, holy, Lord God Almighty.'
claims.extractor_note: Composite imagery drawing on Ezekiel 1 (living creatures/wheels) and Revelation 4:8 (the thrice-holy cry); no single numbered footnote given.

claims.quote: And the redeemed shout “Alleluia!” as the chariot moves onward toward the New Jerusalem.
claims.claim_text: The text quotes the redeemed shouting 'Alleluia!' as the chariot moves toward the New Jerusalem.

claims.quote: every glance is fixed upon Him, every eye beholds His glory whose “visage was so marred more than any man, and His form more than the sons of men.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that Christ's visage 'was so marred more than any man, and His form more than the sons of men.'
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to Isaiah 52:14; no numbered footnote at this instance.

claims.quote: For each there is a crown, bearing his own “new name,” and the inscription, “Holiness to the Lord.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that each crown given to the redeemed bears the wearer's own 'new name' and the inscription 'Holiness to the Lord.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (1118) for 'new name' (Revelation 2:17); 'Holiness to the Lord' echoes Exodus 28:36 / Zechariah 14:20.

claims.quote: each voice is raised in grateful praise: “Unto Him that loved us, and washed us from our sins in His own blood, and hath made us kings and priests unto God and His Father; to Him be glory and dominion forever and ever.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes the redeemed's praise: 'Unto Him that loved us, and washed us from our sins in His own blood, and hath made us kings and priests unto God and His Father; to Him be glory and dominion forever and ever.'

claims.quote: Jesus opens wide the pearly gates, and the nations that have kept the truth enter in. There they behold the Paradise of God, the home of Adam in his innocency.
claims.claim_text: In the vision, Jesus opens the pearly gates of the holy city and the redeemed nations enter and behold the Paradise of God, the original home of Adam in his innocence.

claims.quote: Then that voice, richer than any music that ever fell on mortal ear, is heard, saying, “Your conflict is ended.” “Come, ye blessed of My Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes a voice saying 'Your conflict is ended' and 'Come, ye blessed of My Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.'
claims.extractor_note: The second sentence corresponds to Matthew 25:34; no numbered footnote given here.

claims.quote: Now is fulfilled the Saviour’s prayer for His disciples, “I will that they also whom Thou hast given Me be with Me where I am.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes Jesus' prayer for His disciples, 'I will that they also whom Thou hast given Me be with Me where I am,' as now fulfilled.
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to John 17:24; duplicate citation of the same verse already logged in GC-40-011 (footnote 1092).

claims.quote: “Faultless before the presence of His glory with exceeding joy,” Christ presents to the Father the purchase of His blood, declaring, “Here am I, and the children whom Thou hast given Me.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'Faultless before the presence of His glory with exceeding joy' and has Christ declaring to the Father 'Here am I, and the children whom Thou hast given Me.'
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (1120) for the first phrase, corresponding to Jude 1:24; the second phrase echoes Hebrews 2:13 / Isaiah 8:18.

claims.quote: “Those that Thou gavest Me I have kept.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes Christ declaring 'Those that Thou gavest Me I have kept.'
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to John 17:12; no numbered footnote at this instance.

claims.quote: The Saviour’s joy is in seeing, in the kingdom of glory, the souls that have been saved by His agony and humiliation.
claims.claim_text: The Saviour's joy consists in seeing, in the kingdom of glory, the souls saved through His agony and humiliation.

claims.quote: As Adam discerns the prints of the cruel nails, he does not fall upon the bosom of his Lord, but in humiliation casts himself at His feet, crying, “Worthy, worthy is the Lamb that was slain!”
claims.claim_text: In the vision, Adam, seeing the nail-prints on Christ, casts himself at Christ's feet crying 'Worthy, worthy is the Lamb that was slain!'

claims.quote: With patient humility he bore, for nearly a thousand years, the penalty of transgression.
claims.claim_text: Adam bore the penalty of his transgression, with patient humility, for nearly a thousand years before his death.
claims.extractor_note: Genesis 5:5 gives Adam's total lifespan as 930 years; checkable against that figure.

claims.quote: Faithfully did he repent of his sin, and trust in the merits of the promised Saviour, and he died in the hope of a resurrection.
claims.claim_text: Adam faithfully repented of his sin, trusted in the merits of the promised Saviour, and died in the hope of a resurrection.

claims.quote: He touches the golden harp, and the vaults of heaven echo the triumphant song, “Worthy, worthy, worthy is the Lamb that was slain, and lives again!”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes Adam's song 'Worthy, worthy, worthy is the Lamb that was slain, and lives again!'

claims.quote: This reunion is witnessed by the angels who wept at the fall of Adam, and rejoiced when Jesus, after His resurrection, ascended to heaven, having opened the grave for all who should believe on His name.
claims.claim_text: Jesus ascended to heaven after His resurrection.
claims.extractor_note: References the ascension narrated in Acts 1:9; the angelic weeping/rejoicing is theological and not separately verifiable.

claims.quote: Upon the crystal sea before the throne, that sea of glass as it were mingled with fire,—so resplendent is it with the glory of God,—are gathered the company that have “gotten the victory over the beast, and over his image, and over his mark, and over the number of his name.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that a company gathered on the sea of glass has 'gotten the victory over the beast, and over his image, and over his mark, and over the number of his name.'

claims.quote: With the Lamb upon Mount Zion, “having the harps of God,” they stand, the hundred and forty and four thousand that were redeemed from among men; and there is heard, as the sound of many waters, and as the sound of a great thunder, “the voice of harpers harping with their harps.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that 144,000 redeemed stand with the Lamb on Mount Zion 'having the harps of God,' and 'the voice of harpers harping with their harps' is heard.

claims.quote: And they sing “a new song” before the throne, a song which no man can learn save the hundred and forty and four thousand.
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that the 144,000 sing 'a new song' before the throne, which no other can learn.
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to Revelation 14:3; no numbered footnote at this instance.

claims.quote: These, having been translated from the earth, from among the living, are counted as “the first-fruits unto God and to the Lamb.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that those translated from among the living are counted as 'the first-fruits unto God and to the Lamb.'

claims.quote: “These are they which came out of great tribulation;” they have passed through the time of trouble such as never was since there was a nation; they have endured the anguish of the time of Jacob’s trouble; they have stood without an intercessor through the final outpouring of God’s judgments.
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'These are they which came out of great tribulation,' describing this company as having passed through an unprecedented time of trouble and the 'time of Jacob's trouble' without an intercessor.
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (1124); the quoted clause corresponds to Revelation 7:14; the 'time of Jacob's trouble' and 'without an intercessor' framing is the author's own theological narrative, not itself a direct scriptural quotation.

claims.quote: But they have been delivered, for they have “washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that this company 'washed their robes, and made them white in the blood of the Lamb.'
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to Revelation 7:14 (same verse as GC-40-087).

claims.quote: “In their mouth was found no guile: for they are without fault” before God.
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that 'in their mouth was found no guile: for they are without fault' before God.
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to Revelation 14:5; no numbered footnote at this instance.

claims.quote: “Therefore are they before the throne of God, and serve Him day and night in His temple: and He that sitteth on the throne shall dwell among them.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'Therefore are they before the throne of God, and serve Him day and night in His temple: and He that sitteth on the throne shall dwell among them.'

claims.quote: But “they shall hunger no more, neither thirst any more; neither shall the sun light on them, nor any heat. For the Lamb which is in the midst of the throne shall feed them, and shall lead them unto living fountains of waters: and God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'they shall hunger no more, neither thirst any more... God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes.'

claims.quote: Having been partakers of Christ’s sufferings, they are fitted to be partakers with Him of His glory.
claims.claim_text: The claim that having partaken of Christ's sufferings fits the redeemed to be partakers of His glory.

claims.quote: Millions went down to the grave loaded with infamy, because they steadfastly refused to yield to the deceptive claims of Satan.
claims.claim_text: Millions of God's faithful people throughout history died bearing infamy because they refused to yield to Satan's deceptive claims.
claims.extractor_note: Sweeping, trans-historical statistical claim ('millions') about persecuted believers across all ages; effectively unquantifiable but stated as fact, flagged per pipeline rule that statistics require verification.

claims.quote: But now “God is judge Himself.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'God is judge Himself' to describe the reversal of earthly judgments.
claims.extractor_note: Footnote (1127); corresponds to Psalm 50:6, duplicate citation of the same verse already logged in GC-40-029 (footnote 1101).

claims.quote: “The rebuke of His people shall He take away.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'The rebuke of His people shall He take away.'

claims.quote: “They shall call them, The holy people, The redeemed of the Lord.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'They shall call them, The holy people, The redeemed of the Lord.'
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to Isaiah 62:12; no numbered footnote at this instance.

claims.quote: He hath appointed “to give unto them beauty for ashes, the oil of joy for mourning, the garment of praise for the spirit of heaviness.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes 'to give unto them beauty for ashes, the oil of joy for mourning, the garment of praise for the spirit of heaviness.'

claims.quote: On earth they were “destitute, afflicted, tormented.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes that on earth the redeemed were 'destitute, afflicted, tormented.'
claims.extractor_note: Corresponds to Hebrews 11:37; no numbered footnote at this instance.

claims.quote: until the anthem swells through the vaults of heaven, “Salvation to our God which sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb.” And all the inhabitants of heaven respond in the ascription, “Amen: Blessing, and glory, and wisdom, and thanksgiving, and honor, and power, and might, be unto our God forever and ever.”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes the anthem 'Salvation to our God which sitteth upon the throne, and unto the Lamb' and the response 'Amen: Blessing, and glory, and wisdom, and thanksgiving, and honor, and power, and might, be unto our God forever and ever.'

claims.quote: The plan of redemption will not be fully understood, even when the ransomed see as they are seen and know as they are known; but through the eternal ages, new truth will continually unfold to the wondering and delighted mind.
claims.claim_text: The plan of redemption will never be fully understood, even in eternity; new truth about it will continually unfold.

claims.quote: As the nations of the saved look upon their Redeemer, and behold the eternal glory of the Father shining in His countenance; as they behold His throne, which is from everlasting to everlasting, and know that His kingdom is to have no end, they break forth in rapturous song, “Worthy, worthy is the Lamb that was slain, and hath redeemed us to God by His own most precious blood!”
claims.claim_text: The text quotes the redeemed nations singing 'Worthy, worthy is the Lamb that was slain, and hath redeemed us to God by His own most precious blood!'

claims.quote: The cross of Christ will be the science and the song of the redeemed through all eternity.
claims.claim_text: The cross of Christ will be the science and the song of the redeemed through all eternity.

claims.quote: The mystery of the cross explains all other mysteries. In the light that streams from Calvary, the attributes of God which had filled us with fear and awe appear beautiful and attractive.
claims.claim_text: The mystery of the cross explains all other mysteries, and in the light from Calvary the attributes of God appear beautiful and attractive rather than fearsome.

claims.quote: It will be seen that He who is infinite in wisdom could devise no plan for our salvation except the sacrifice of His Son.
claims.claim_text: God, infinite in wisdom, could devise no plan for human salvation except the sacrifice of His Son.

claims.quote: And such is the value of the soul that the Father is satisfied with the price paid; and Christ Himself, beholding the fruits of His great sacrifice, is satisfied.
claims.claim_text: The value of the human soul is such that the Father is satisfied with the price paid for it, and Christ is satisfied beholding the fruits of His sacrifice.
